component.description: 2.95V to 6V Input, 3A Output, 2MHz, Synchronous Step-Down SWIFT™ Converter

electrical_characteristics.conditions: No voltage hysteresis, rising and falling
VEN = 0 V, TA = 25°C, 2.95 V ≤ VVIN ≤ 6 V
VVSENSE = 0.9 V, VVIN = 5 V, TA = 25°C, RT = 400 kΩ
-2 µA < ICOMP < 2 µA, VCOMP = 1 V, VVSENSE = 0.4 V

electrical_characteristics.parameter: Switching frequency range using RT mode
electrical_characteristics.raw_name: Switching frequency range using RT mode

electrical_characteristics.parameter: Switching frequency range using CLK mode
electrical_characteristics.raw_name: Switching frequency range using CLK mode

electrical_characteristics.parameter: RT/CLK falling edge to PH rising edge delay
electrical_characteristics.raw_name: RT/CLK falling edge to PH rising edge delay
electrical_characteristics.conditions: fSW = 500 kHz with RRT resistor in series
Measured at 50% points on PH, IOUT = 3
Measured at 50% points on PH, VVIN = 5 V, IOUT = 0 A
Prior to skipping off pulses, (VBOOT – VPH) = 2.95 V, IOUT = 3

electrical_characteristics.parameter: SS discharge current (UVLO, EN, thermal fault)
electrical_characteristics.raw_name: SS discharge current (UVLO, EN, thermal fault)

electrical_characteristics.parameter: Minimum input voltage for valid output
electrical_characteristics.raw_name: Minimum input voltage for valid output

pin_definitions.description: Input supply voltage, 2.95 V to 6 V.
Power ground. This pin should be electrically connected directly to the power pad under the device.
Analog ground should be electrically connected to GND close to the device.
Inverting node of the transconductance (gm) error amplifier.
Error amplifier output, and input to the output switch current comparator. Connect frequency compensation components to this pin.
Resistor Timing or External Clock input pin.
Slow-start. An external capacitor connected to this pin sets the output voltage rise time.
The source of the internal high-side power MOSFET, and drain of the internal low-side (synchronous) rectifier MOSFET.

A bootstrap capacitor is required between BOOT and PH. If the voltage on this capacitor is below the minimum required by the BOOT UVLO, the output is forced to switch off until the capacitor is refreshed.
An open drain output, asserts low if output voltage is low due to thermal shutdown, overcurrent, over/under-voltage or EN shut down.
Enable pin, internal pull-up current source. Pull below 1.2 V to disable. Float to enable. Can be used to set the on/off threshold (adjust UVLO) with two additional resistors.
GND pin should be connected to the exposed power pad for proper operation. This power pad should be connected to any internal PCB ground plane using multiple vias for good thermal performance.
